title: frangula rubra subsp. modocensis
intro: Frangula rubra subsp. modocensis, also known as Modoc Buckthorn, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the Rhamnaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural laxative and having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Frangula rubra subsp. modocensis include supporting digestive health, reducing inflammation, and potentially aiding in the management of certain gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Modoc Buckthorn can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used in traditional folk medicine to support overall well-being.
